extension.valueMarkdown:
Public health is notified. Contact investigation initiated. Robert is started on RIPE therapy (rifampin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide, ethambutol). After clinical improvement he's discharged to outpatient directly observed therapy (DOT) at the affiliated TB clinic.

Two sites, one health system, one state (IL). This tests Permission Ticket constraints on period (only the investigation window) and scopes (public health may only need specific resource types like Condition, DiagnosticReport, Observation for lab results).

resource.description: DOT adherence log summary confirming 100% compliance with directly observed therapy throughout the treatment course, covering both intensive and early continuation phases.

extension.valueMarkdown: RN Patricia Gomez called Robert at home two days after discharge to assess his clinical status and confirm his upcoming appointment. Robert reported he was tolerating his medications, though he noted orange discoloration of his urine which she reassured him is an expected effect of rifampin.

reasonCode.text: 2-week follow-up after RIPE therapy initiation; DOT enrollment; baseline visual acuity for ethambutol monitoring

extension.valueMarkdown: Robert presented for his first outpatient visit at the UI Health Ambulatory TB Clinic, two weeks after starting RIPE therapy. He reported persistent but improving cough, now less productive.
Dr. Okafor conducted a brief telemedicine check-in with Robert via the UI Health video platform.

reasonCode.text: Interim hepatic function monitoring during RIPE therapy for pulmonary tuberculosis

extension.valueMarkdown: Robert presented to the TB clinic for a scheduled lab draw only. He was seen briefly by RN Patricia Gomez who confirmed no new symptoms and observed his morning RIPE dose.

reasonCode.text: Lab results review, inpatient culture results

extension.valueMarkdown: RN Patricia Gomez called Robert to discuss results from the February 19 lab draw and to relay the final inpatient sputum culture results. LFTs remain stable and acceptable — no dose adjustments needed.

reasonCode.text: 6-week follow-up, sputum conversion monitoring, repeat imaging

extension.valueMarkdown: Robert presented for his 6-week follow-up, now approximately 6 weeks into RIPE therapy. He reported significant clinical improvement.
Dr. Okafor called Robert to discuss the sputum culture result from the February 5 specimen.
Robert presented for his 8-week follow-up. He continues to feel well.
Robert presented for a scheduled lab draw ahead of his 10-week follow-up visit. He was seen briefly by RN Gomez who observed his RIPE dose and confirmed no new complaints.
Dr. Okafor called Robert to relay the final sputum culture result from the March 5 specimen.

Robert presented for his 10-week follow-up, a key milestone visit marking the end of the intensive phase and transition to the continuation phase of TB treatment. He is in notably better clinical condition than at presentation.
RN Patricia Gomez called Robert one week after the phase transition to assess tolerance of the simplified two-drug regimen. Robert reported feeling well — no new symptoms.
Robert presented for his 12-week follow-up, now 2 weeks into the continuation phase. He continues to do well clinically.

resource.conclusion: Sputum AFB smear positive, 1+ — decreased from 2+ at admission, indicating decreasing organism burden.

resource.conclusion: AST 35 U/L, ALT 38 U/L — mildly elevated from admission baseline (28/32) but within acceptable limits for continued RIPE therapy (less than 3× ULN).
Sodium 139, potassium 4.1, creatinine 1.0 — normal renal function and electrolytes. Random glucose 168 mg/dL, elevated, consistent with known type 2 diabetes.

resource.conclusion: AST 33 U/L, ALT 36 U/L — stable and within acceptable limits for continued RIPE therapy. No evidence of drug-induced hepatotoxicity.

code.text: Mycobacterial Culture and Drug Susceptibility Testing, Sputum

resource.conclusion: Sputum mycobacterial culture positive for Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Drug susceptibility testing confirms pan-susceptibility to all first-line agents (rifampin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide, ethambutol, streptomycin). Supports continuation of current RIPE regimen.

coding.display: Microscopic observation [Identifier] in Sputum by Acid fast stain

resource.conclusion: Sputum AFB smear NEGATIVE — smear conversion confirmed at 6 weeks of RIPE therapy. Previously positive (1+ at 2-week visit).

coding.display: CBC panel - Blood by Automated count
XR Chest PA and Lateral upright

resource.conclusion: Interval decrease in size of right upper lobe cavitary lesion, now approximately 2.5 cm (previously 3.5 cm on 01/22/2026). Surrounding infiltrate has partially cleared. Right hilar lymph nodes appear less prominent. No new infiltrates or effusions. Impression: Improving pulmonary tuberculosis on treatment.
Uric acid 8.9 mg/dL, mildly elevated consistent with pyrazinamide effect. Patient asymptomatic, no gouty symptoms.

resource.conclusion: Sputum mycobacterial culture (specimen collected February 5, 2026) returned negative — no growth of Mycobacterium tuberculosis after 42 days of incubation. This is the first of two consecutive negative cultures needed to document sputum culture conversion.

Uric acid 8.6 mg/dL, mildly elevated. Expected side effect of pyrazinamide; asymptomatic. Will resolve after PZA discontinuation.

note.text: 12-week follow-up (04/16/2026): Continuation phase, 2 weeks in. Clinically well, sputum AFB smear negative. Prior sputum culture conversion confirmed. Weight recovered to 170 lbs (from 162 lbs nadir). LFTs normalized after PZA discontinuation. Continuing rifampin 600 mg + isoniazid 300 mg three times weekly with pyridoxine. 100% DOT compliance. Anticipated treatment completion late July 2026.
Patient reports boredom and isolation during home confinement for TB treatment. Denies depressed mood or hopelessness. Maintains family contact by phone. Will continue to monitor at future visits.
Uric acid 8.9 mg/dL, mildly elevated and consistent with pyrazinamide effect. Patient asymptomatic, no gout symptoms. Expected to resolve after pyrazinamide discontinuation at end of intensive phase.
Improving — Hgb 12.8 g/dL (up from 11.2 g/dL at admission). Likely anemia of chronic disease/inflammation, resolving with TB treatment response.

medicationCodeableConcept.text: Ethambutol 1200 mg daily (3 x 400 mg tablets)

dosageInstruction.text: Take 3 tablets (1200 mg total) by mouth once daily. DISCONTINUED 04/02/2026 — completing 2-month intensive phase, transitioning to continuation phase.

note.text: Discontinued 04/02/2026 — completing 2-month intensive phase of TB treatment, transitioning to continuation phase (rifampin + isoniazid only). No ethambutol-related visual toxicity identified.

medicationCodeableConcept.text: Pyrazinamide 1500 mg daily (3 x 500 mg tablets)

dosageInstruction.text: Take 3 tablets (1500 mg total) by mouth once daily. DISCONTINUED 04/02/2026 — completing 2-month intensive phase, transitioning to continuation phase.

note.text: Discontinued 04/02/2026 — completing 2-month intensive phase of TB treatment, transitioning to continuation phase (rifampin + isoniazid only).
